import io
import string
import list

fn is_digit(_ b: Int) -> Bool
  b >= 48 and b <= 57
end

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut i = 0
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut current = 0
  mut has_digit = false
  mut negative = false
  while i <= n
    mut is_end = false
    mut b = 0
    if i == n
      is_end = true
    else
      match string.byte_at(s, i)
      when Some(x)
        b = x
      when None
        is_end = true
      end
    end
    if is_end
      if has_digit
        if negative
          result = list.push(result, 0 - current)
        else
          result = list.push(result, current)
        end
        current = 0
        has_digit = false
        negative = false
      end
    else if is_digit(b)
      current = current * 10 + (b - 48)
      has_digit = true
    else if b == 45 and not has_digit
      negative = true
    else
      if has_digit
        if negative
          result = list.push(result, 0 - current)
        else
          result = list.push(result, current)
        end
        current = 0
        has_digit = false
        negative = false
      end
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  result
end

fn find_index(_ xs: List<Int>, _ target: Int) -> Option<Int>
  list.index_of(xs, target)
end

fn main() -> Unit
  let line1 = match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    s
  when None
    ""
  end
  let line2 = match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    s
  when None
    ""
  end
  let xs = parse_ints(line1)
  let targets = parse_ints(line2)
  let target = match list.get(targets, 0)
  when Some(t)
    t
  when None
    0
  end
  match find_index(xs, target)
  when Some(i)
    print("index=#{i}")
  when None
    print("not_found")
  end
end
